prevalence.trend: stable; detection increases with routine calcium testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; morbidity from stones and bone loss

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Elevated calcium with inappropriately high PTH; assess vitamin D, renal function, bone density; imaging for surgical planning

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with surgical cure when indicated

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Lab testing widely available; parathyroid imaging in reference centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Parathyroidectomy for symptomatic or meeting criteria; monitoring and medical management for mild cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Surgical access and delayed referral in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear diagnostic criteria and effective surgical treatment
